character_dossiers.name: the captain
character_dossiers.biography: Led the boat crew during a perilous landing, ordered actions to avoid gunfire, and retrieved supplies from the wrecked boat.

character_dossiers.name: the squire
character_dossiers.biography: Assisted in coordinating the boat's defense, handed over a gun to Trelawney, and comforted the dying Tom Redruth.

character_dossiers.name: Jim
character_dossiers.biography: Hear the gunshot but did not participate in the immediate action, observed the boat's sinking.

character_dossiers.name: Redruth
character_dossiers.biography: Part of the boat crew, survived the sinking, and was later found dead after being carried to the log-house.

character_dossiers.name: Trelawney
character_dossiers.biography: Identified as the dead shot, exchanged guns with the captain, and remained calm during the conflict.

character_dossiers.name: Gray
character_dossiers.biography: Received a cutlass from the squire, demonstrated combat readiness, and fought alongside the group.

character_dossiers.name: Job Anderson
character_dossiers.biography: Led the mutineers attacking the stockade, was shot and killed during the confrontation.

character_dossiers.name: the boatswain
character_dossiers.biography: Part of the mutineers, participated in the attack on the stockade, and was among those killed.

character_dossiers.name: Tom Redruth
character_dossiers.biography: Died after being carried to the log-house, remained silent and stoic until his death.

character_dossiers.name: the old gamekeeper
character_dossiers.biography: Carried to the log-house after being wounded, died without protest, and was described as a silent, loyal servant.
